items.question: Who benefits from experiment IDs and a model registry?
items.answer: ML engineers, researchers, and platform teams benefit: engineers trace production issues to training runs, researchers compare sweeps fairly, and platform teams enforce promotion policies with audit-ready lineage.

items.question: What is an experiment ID in practice?
items.answer: An experiment ID (run ID) is a unique identifier the tracking system assigns to one training execution, used as the key to fetch that run's parameters, metrics, tags, and artifacts.

items.question: When should you register a model in the registry instead of leaving it as a run?
items.answer: Register after validation when an artifact is a serious deployment candidate—not for every exploratory sweep run; use the registry when you need named versions, stages, and governed promotion.

items.question: Where do tags and run IDs live relative to your training code?
items.answer: They live in the experiment tracking backend (MLflow, W&B, etc.); your training script creates a run, receives an ID, and sets tags via the tool's API while logging params and metrics to the same run.

items.question: Why compare runs by ID rather than by notebook name alone?
items.answer: Notebook or run names collide and omit structured params and metrics; comparing by run ID pulls the exact logged configuration and scores from the tracking store for an apples-to-apples decision.

items.question: How do you compare two training runs fairly?
items.answer: Select two run IDs logged with the same metric keys, evaluation split, and data version; filter with tags if needed, align params side by side, and confirm seeds or reproducibility metadata before picking a winner.

items.question: Experiment ID vs tag: when do you use each?
items.answer: Use an experiment ID to retrieve one specific run and its artifacts precisely; use tags to label and filter groups of runs (e.g. all `candidate` runs). IDs are unique keys; tags are shared labels.

items.question: Experiment storage vs model registry: what is the difference?
items.answer: Experiment storage holds every run with logs and artifacts for exploration; the model registry holds explicitly registered, versioned models with promotion stages (Staging, Production) and lineage to a source run ID.
